entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A unique food legume [Cajanus cajan (L.) Millsp.] with a partial outcrossing nature

entities.name: OUTCROSSING
entities.description: The partial (20-30%) outcrossing nature of pigeonpea

entities.name: COMMERCIAL HYBRIDS
entities.description: Hybrids bred for commercial agricultural use

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC-NUCLEAR MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A stable male-sterility system (CMS) derived from an interspecific cross

entities.name: INTERSPECIFIC CROSS
entities.description: A breeding method involving a cross between different species

entities.name: CAJANUS SERICEUS
entities.description: A wild relative of pigeonpea used in an interspecific cross

entities.name: CULTIVAR
entities.description: A cultivated variety of pigeonpea used in an interspecific cross

entities.name: MATURITY DURATION
entities.description: The time to maturity, categorized as early, medium, or late

entities.name: FERTILITY RESTORERS
entities.description: Lines selected to restore fertility in CMS hybrids

entities.name: MALE-STERILITY MAINTAINERS
entities.description: Lines selected to maintain male sterility in CMS lines

entities.name: HISTOLOGICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Microscopic analysis of tissues to identify causes of male sterility

entities.name: VACUOLATION OF GROWING TETRADS
entities.description: A cellular condition identified as a primary cause of male sterility

entities.name: PERSISTENCE OF TETRAD WALL
entities.description: A cellular condition identified as a primary cause of male sterility

entities.name: MALE STERILITY
entities.description: The trait of being unable to produce functional pollen

entities.name: GENETIC STUDIES
entities.description: Analysis of the inheritance and genes controlling fertility restoration

entities.name: DOMINANT GENES
entities.description: Two dominant genes that control fertility restoration in hybrids

entities.name: INHIBITORY GENE ACTION
entities.description: A genetic mechanism where one gene inhibits the action of another

entities.name: FERTILITY RESTORATION
entities.description: The trait of regaining fertility in male-sterile hybrids

entities.name: EXPERIMENTAL HYBRIDS
entities.description: Specific hybrid combinations such as TK 030003 and ICPH 2307 tested for heterosis

entities.name: STANDARD HETEROSIS
entities.description: The degree of superiority of hybrids over their parents

entities.name: MULTILOCATION TRIALS
entities.description: Field tests conducted across multiple locations to evaluate hybrid performance
